name: Dashboard 'Money owed' panel has no stray dashed divider in the empty state
description: Empty-state Outstanding panel: the dashed border-top container that holds invoice rows must NOT render when items.length === 0. Otherwise an orphan dashed line appears under the empty-state copy and visually disconnects the message.
preconditions: Account has zero invoices and zero outstanding balance

steps.expect: Outstanding panel shows 'Money owed to you' label, '$0' amount, and the empty-state copy 'No invoices yet — once you bill a job, it'll show up here.'
When items is empty, the wrapping <div> with border-top:1px dashed is NOT in the DOM at all (gated by items.length > 0); when populated, the divider returns above the rows

evaluationCriteria.check: Empty-state copy sits as the visual bottom of the panel; no orphan horizontal line below it
Panel reads as a single empty unit, not as 'header + body + (mystery section)'
The same panel transitions cleanly when first invoice lands — divider appears above the new row
DOM tree is shorter in the empty state; screen reader doesn't announce a 'separator' that has no items below it
